note: Mathlib/Probability/Combinatorics/BinomialRandomGraph/Defs.lean:46 def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) — the G(V,p) binomial random graph measure; file docstring: 'This is usually called the Erdős–Rényi model' (named 'binomial random graph' in-file to distinguish from Erdős/Rényi's original uniform G(n,M) model, but this is the object the Wikidata concept denotes).
verify_note: Decl is real (confirmed in declaration-data.json and Mathlib/Probability/Combinatorics/BinomialRandomGraph/Defs.lean) but 'exact' overclaims: the file's own 'Historical note' calls the Erdős–Rényi identification 'historically inaccurate' since binomialRandom is the Gilbert G(V,p) model, not Erdős/Rényi's original uniform G(n,M) construction, and Wikidata Q605807 (confirmed label 'Erdős–Rényi model') plus its enwiki article explicitly denote TWO closely related models (G(n,M) and G(n,p)/Erdős–Rényi–Gilbert) as one bundled topic — this decl formalizes only one of the two, so the proposer's own source contradicts 'exact'; should be 'related' at best.

note: Directory exists with real content: KraftMcMillan.lean (Kraft-McMillan inequality for prefix/uniquely-decodable codes) and UniquelyDecodable.lean (def UniquelyDecodable + epsilon_not_mem, flatten_injective); confirmed present in catalog/data/hierarchy.json as Mathlib.InformationTheory.Coding.KraftMcMillan / .UniquelyDecodable. Field-level container, not a single-theorem match.
verify_note: Q602136 confirmed label 'coding theory' on Wikidata (enwiki sitelink 'Coding theory'); Mathlib/InformationTheory/Coding is a real directory (KraftMcMillan.lean, UniquelyDecodable.lean, both read in full) with genuine coding-theory content (Kraft-McMillan inequality, uniquely decodable code definitions), and appears as a node in hierarchy.json's nested tree (libraries.Mathlib.modules.InformationTheory.sub.Coding, aggregate n_decls=4 across its two files) — legitimate field-level container, no overclaim.
